description:
Router._sendEth(address,uint256) (src/Router.sol#237-240) sends eth to arbitrary user
	Dangerous calls:
	- (ok,None) = to.call{value: amount}() (src/Router.sol#238)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. Router._sendEth is `private` and reached from exactly one site (createToken, the creation-fee push) with `factoryContract.treasury()` — a factory-configured address, NEVER caller-supplied (spec §4.1). Guarded by nonReentrant + CEI. Not an arbitrary send. contracts.md §2.4/§5.6.

description:
CreatorVault.claim(address) (src/CreatorVault.sol#101-109) sends eth to arbitrary user
	Dangerous calls:
	- (ok,None) = creator.call{value: amount}() (src/CreatorVault.sol#105)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE (§12.63). CreatorVault.claim(creator) sends the creator's OWN accrued balance (balanceOf[creator]) to that SAME `creator` address — the destination is the fee-earner, never caller-supplied or arbitrary. Permissionless in WHO calls (pays gas), FIXED in WHERE funds go (mirrors LPFeeVault.collect → fixed treasury, an accepted pattern). nonReentrant + CEI (balance zeroed before the send). A hostile creator only reverts its own claim (retriable), never a trade.

description:
Reentrancy in V3Migrator._mintAndSettle(V3Migrator.Ctx) (src/V3Migrator.sol#411-475):
	External call allowing reentrancy:
	- _mint(m) (src/V3Migrator.sol#457)
		- (m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.used0,m.used1) = INonfungiblePositionManager(positionManager).mint(INonfungiblePositionManager.MintParams({token0:m.token0,token1:m.token1,fee:FEE_TIER,tickLower:TICK_LOWER,tickUpper:TICK_UPPER,amount0Desired:m.amt0Desired,amount1Desired:m.amt1Desired,amount0Min:m.amt0Min,amount1Min:m.amt1Min,recipient:vault,deadline:block.timestamp})) (src/V3Migrator.sol#480-495)
	Balance read before the call:
	- tokenBal = IERC20(c.token).balanceOf(address(this)) (src/V3Migrator.sol#422)
	Possible stale balance used after the call in a condition:
	- m.liquidity == 0 (src/V3Migrator.sol#458)
		- stale variable `REF_487`
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. The flagged external call is NPM.mint() to the real, trusted, immutable Uniswap NonfungiblePositionManager (§12.28 pinned address, asserted at deploy). The 'stale' tokenBal/wethBal feed only amount0/1Desired and the post-mint `m.liquidity==0` guard (InsufficientLiquidityMinted — defense-in-depth). LaunchToken & WETH have no transfer hooks; the only reentrancy surface (the pool swap callback) is closed by the `_activePool` guard in _arbToTarget, which has fully returned before mint. No attacker-controlled reentry. contracts.md §2.5/§5.4. (§12.69 re-triage: the added registerCreator call to the trusted immutable LPFeeVault is in-trace after _mint and after the m.liquidity==0 guard — a set-once, migrator-gated write to a trusted contract — so the reentrancy analysis is unchanged.)

description:
Reentrancy in V3Migrator._mintAndSettle(V3Migrator.Ctx) (src/V3Migrator.sol#411-475):
	External call allowing reentrancy:
	- _mint(m) (src/V3Migrator.sol#457)
		- (m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.used0,m.used1) = INonfungiblePositionManager(positionManager).mint(INonfungiblePositionManager.MintParams({token0:m.token0,token1:m.token1,fee:FEE_TIER,tickLower:TICK_LOWER,tickUpper:TICK_UPPER,amount0Desired:m.amt0Desired,amount1Desired:m.amt1Desired,amount0Min:m.amt0Min,amount1Min:m.amt1Min,recipient:vault,deadline:block.timestamp})) (src/V3Migrator.sol#480-495)
	Balance read before the call:
	- wethBal = IERC20(weth_).balanceOf(address(this)) (src/V3Migrator.sol#423)
	Possible stale balance used after the call in a condition:
	- m.liquidity == 0 (src/V3Migrator.sol#458)
		- stale variable `REF_487`
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. The flagged external call is NPM.mint() to the real, trusted, immutable Uniswap NonfungiblePositionManager (§12.28 pinned address, asserted at deploy). The 'stale' tokenBal/wethBal feed only amount0/1Desired and the post-mint `m.liquidity==0` guard (InsufficientLiquidityMinted — defense-in-depth). LaunchToken & WETH have no transfer hooks; the only reentrancy surface (the pool swap callback) is closed by the `_activePool` guard in _arbToTarget, which has fully returned before mint. No attacker-controlled reentry. contracts.md §2.5/§5.4. (§12.69 re-triage: the added registerCreator call to the trusted immutable LPFeeVault is in-trace after _mint and after the m.liquidity==0 guard — a set-once, migrator-gated write to a trusted contract — so the reentrancy analysis is unchanged.)

description:
V3Migrator._mintAndSettle(V3Migrator.Ctx) (src/V3Migrator.sol#411-475) uses a dangerous strict equality:
	- m.liquidity == 0 (src/V3Migrator.sol#458)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. `m.liquidity==0` (revert InsufficientLiquidityMinted) and `budget==0` (revert ArbBudgetExceeded) are exact zero-guards on integers derived from trusted V3/NPM returns and in-contract math — strict equality is the correct and intended semantics, not a dangerous balance/timestamp equality.

description:
V3Migrator._arbStep(V3Migrator.Ctx,uint256,uint256) (src/V3Migrator.sol#352-389) uses a dangerous strict equality:
	- budget == 0 (src/V3Migrator.sol#380)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. `m.liquidity==0` (revert InsufficientLiquidityMinted) and `budget==0` (revert ArbBudgetExceeded) are exact zero-guards on integers derived from trusted V3/NPM returns and in-contract math — strict equality is the correct and intended semantics, not a dangerous balance/timestamp equality.

description:
Reentrancy in BondingCurve.buy(address,address,address,uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#174-267):
	External calls:
	- ICurveFactory(factory).recordEthDelta(int256(net)) (src/BondingCurve.sol#239)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- _realEthReserves = newRealEth (src/BondingCurve.sol#252)
	BondingCurve._realEthReserves (src/BondingCurve.sol#123) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- BondingCurve.quoteBuy(uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#377-409)
	- BondingCurve.reserves() (src/BondingCurve.sol#422-429)
	- phase = Phase.ReadyToGraduate (src/BondingCurve.sol#257)
	BondingCurve.phase (src/BondingCurve.sol#131) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- BondingCurve.constructor() (src/BondingCurve.sol#138-169)
	- BondingCurve.phase (src/BondingCurve.sol#131)
	- BondingCurve.quoteBuy(uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#377-409)
	- BondingCurve.quoteSell(uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#412-419)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. The pre-write external call is to the trusted, immutable factory (CurveFactory.createToken→migrator.initializePool; BondingCurve.buy→factory.recordEthDelta). initializePool only touches the V3 factory/pool and recordEthDelta is a bounded accumulator — neither calls back into the flagged contract. Both entrypoints are nonReentrant (createToken is onlyRouter behind the nonReentrant Router; buy is nonReentrant) so cross-function reentry is impossible. State written after the call is pre-computed before it. (BondingCurve.buy: recordEthDelta is an interaction slightly ahead of the effects block — acknowledged CEI-ordering nuance, safe because the callee is trusted and cannot reenter.) contracts.md §5.4.

description:
Reentrancy in CurveFactory.createToken(address,string,string,bytes32,string) (src/CurveFactory.sol#259-291):
	External calls:
	- pool = IV3Migrator(migrator).initializePool(token) (src/CurveFactory.sol#279)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- ++ tokenCounter (src/CurveFactory.sol#286)
	CurveFactory.tokenCounter (src/CurveFactory.sol#151) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- CurveFactory.createToken(address,string,string,bytes32,string) (src/CurveFactory.sol#259-291)
	- CurveFactory.tokenCounter (src/CurveFactory.sol#151)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. The pre-write external call is to the trusted, immutable factory (CurveFactory.createToken→migrator.initializePool; BondingCurve.buy→factory.recordEthDelta). initializePool only touches the V3 factory/pool and recordEthDelta is a bounded accumulator — neither calls back into the flagged contract. Both entrypoints are nonReentrant (createToken is onlyRouter behind the nonReentrant Router; buy is nonReentrant) so cross-function reentry is impossible. State written after the call is pre-computed before it. (BondingCurve.buy: recordEthDelta is an interaction slightly ahead of the effects block — acknowledged CEI-ordering nuance, safe because the callee is trusted and cannot reenter.) contracts.md §5.4.

description: V3Migrator.migrate(address).c (src/V3Migrator.sol#292) is a local variable never initialized
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. `Ctx memory c` and `MintLocals memory m` are structs whose fields are all populated before first read; `wethArbSpent` and `refund` are uint256 intentionally starting at 0 (arb accumulator / no-refund default). Standard Slither memory-struct false positive.

description: Router._deploy(string,string,bytes32,string) (src/Router.sol#124-129) ignores return value by (token,curve,None) = factoryContract.createToken(msg.sender,name,symbol,metadataHash,metadataUri) (src/Router.sol#128)
disposition: REVIEWED — INTENTIONAL. Router create/buy/sell/quote + _deploy discard tuple fields that are either re-emitted elsewhere (pool addr in factory TokenCreated; acceptedEthGross/fee in curve Trade) or not needed by the deliberately-thin Router; no security decision depends on a dropped value. V3Migrator slot0() partial-destructures to read only sqrtPriceX96/tick (rest of slot0 genuinely unused). V3Migrator pool.swap() return is unneeded — spend is measured via WETH balance delta and the swap is price-capped to targetSqrt. BondingCurve.graduate discards migrate()'s (tokenId,liquidity); the migrator emits Graduated. contracts.md §2.4/§2.5.

description: LPFeeVault.collect(uint256) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#114-131) ignores return value by (None,None,token0,token1,None,None,None,None,None,None,None,None) = positionManager.positions(tokenId) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#120)
disposition: INTENTIONAL (§12.69). LPFeeVault.collect partial-destructures NonfungiblePositionManager.positions(tokenId), reading ONLY token0/token1 to identify the graduated pool's two legs for the 50/50 split; every other position field (liquidity, feeGrowth*, tokensOwed*, …) is deliberately unused. Read-only view on the trusted immutable NPM (§12.28); no state or value depends on a discarded field. Same class/rationale as the existing V3Migrator slot0() partial-destructure dispositions.

description:
CreatorVault.claim(address).creator (src/CreatorVault.sol#101) lacks a zero-check on :
		- (ok,None) = creator.call{value: amount}() (src/CreatorVault.sol#105)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE (§12.63). A zero `creator` is unreachable-with-value: deposit(creator) reverts ZeroAddress on a zero creator, so balanceOf[address(0)] is always 0, and claim() guards the send with `if (amount != 0)` — a zero-balance claim sends nothing. No ETH can reach address(0). A redundant zero-check would only add bytecode to the minimal-surface vault (spec §6.6).

description:
V3Migrator._arbStep(V3Migrator.Ctx,uint256,uint256) (src/V3Migrator.sol#352-389) has external calls inside a loop: IUniswapV3Pool(c.pool).swap(address(this),zeroForOne,_toInt256(budget),c.targetSqrt,) (src/V3Migrator.sol#383)
	Calls stack containing the loop:
		V3Migrator.migrate(address)
		V3Migrator._arbToTarget(V3Migrator.Ctx)
disposition: INTENTIONAL / ACKNOWLEDGED. The bounded arb-back loop (MAX_ARB_ITERATIONS) inherently queries slot0()/balances and calls swap() per iteration — iterative price correction of a pre-seeded pool (spec §6.3.2). Bounded iteration count → no unbounded-gas griefing; every call targets the trusted V3 pool / WETH. Budget exhaustion reverts cleanly (curve stays ReadyToGraduate, retriable) — never a hostile mint.

description:
V3Migrator._arbStep(V3Migrator.Ctx,uint256,uint256) (src/V3Migrator.sol#352-389) has external calls inside a loop: wethArbSpent + (wethBefore - IERC20(weth).balanceOf(address(this))) (src/V3Migrator.sol#386)
	Calls stack containing the loop:
		V3Migrator.migrate(address)
		V3Migrator._arbToTarget(V3Migrator.Ctx)
disposition: INTENTIONAL / ACKNOWLEDGED. The bounded arb-back loop (MAX_ARB_ITERATIONS) inherently queries slot0()/balances and calls swap() per iteration — iterative price correction of a pre-seeded pool (spec §6.3.2). Bounded iteration count → no unbounded-gas griefing; every call targets the trusted V3 pool / WETH. Budget exhaustion reverts cleanly (curve stays ReadyToGraduate, retriable) — never a hostile mint.

description:
Reentrancy in V3Migrator.migrate(address) (src/V3Migrator.sol#289-327):
	External calls:
	- IWETH9(weth).deposit{value: msg.value}() (src/V3Migrator.sol#309)
	- _arbToTarget(c) (src/V3Migrator.sol#319)
		- IUniswapV3Pool(c.pool).swap(address(this),zeroForOne,_toInt256(budget),c.targetSqrt,) (src/V3Migrator.sol#383)
	- (tokenId,liquidity) = _mintAndSettle(c) (src/V3Migrator.sol#326)
		- (m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.used0,m.used1) = INonfungiblePositionManager(positionManager).mint(INonfungiblePositionManager.MintParams({token0:m.token0,token1:m.token1,fee:FEE_TIER,tickLower:TICK_LOWER,tickUpper:TICK_UPPER,amount0Desired:m.amt0Desired,amount1Desired:m.amt1Desired,amount0Min:m.amt0Min,amount1Min:m.amt1Min,recipient:vault,deadline:block.timestamp})) (src/V3Migrator.sol#480-495)
		- ILPFeeVault(vault).registerCreator(m.tokenId,c.creator) (src/V3Migrator.sol#467)
	External calls sending eth:
	- IWETH9(weth).deposit{value: msg.value}() (src/V3Migrator.sol#309)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- Graduated(c.token,c.pool,m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.wethInPosition,m.tokensInPosition,c.gradFee,tx.origin,IBondingCurve(msg.sender).CALLER_REWARD(),m.tokensBurned,m.wethDust) (src/V3Migrator.sol#521-533)
		- (tokenId,liquidity) = _mintAndSettle(c) (src/V3Migrator.sol#326)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. Events (Trade/Graduated/PoolInitialized/FeesCollected) are emitted after an external call to a trusted contract; emission ordering affects only log ordering, never funds or control flow. LPFeeVault.collect emits after NPM.collect (NPM trusted, §12.28). (§12.69 re-triage: the added registerCreator call to the trusted immutable LPFeeVault is in-trace after _mint and after the m.liquidity==0 guard — a set-once, migrator-gated write to a trusted contract — so the reentrancy analysis is unchanged.)

description:

	External calls:
	- _mint(m) (src/V3Migrator.sol#457)
		- (m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.used0,m.used1) = INonfungiblePositionManager(positionManager).mint(INonfungiblePositionManager.MintParams({token0:m.token0,token1:m.token1,fee:FEE_TIER,tickLower:TICK_LOWER,tickUpper:TICK_UPPER,amount0Desired:m.amt0Desired,amount1Desired:m.amt1Desired,amount0Min:m.amt0Min,amount1Min:m.amt1Min,recipient:vault,deadline:block.timestamp})) (src/V3Migrator.sol#480-495)
	- ILPFeeVault(vault).registerCreator(m.tokenId,c.creator) (src/V3Migrator.sol#467)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- Graduated(c.token,c.pool,m.tokenId,m.liquidity,m.wethInPosition,m.tokensInPosition,c.gradFee,tx.origin,IBondingCurve(msg.sender).CALLER_REWARD(),m.tokensBurned,m.wethDust) (src/V3Migrator.sol#521-533)
		- _settleDust(c,m) (src/V3Migrator.sol#473)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE. Events (Trade/Graduated/PoolInitialized/FeesCollected) are emitted after an external call to a trusted contract; emission ordering affects only log ordering, never funds or control flow. LPFeeVault.collect emits after NPM.collect (NPM trusted, §12.28). (§12.69 re-triage: the added registerCreator call to the trusted immutable LPFeeVault is in-trace after _mint and after the m.liquidity==0 guard — a set-once, migrator-gated write to a trusted contract — so the reentrancy analysis is unchanged.)

description:
Reentrancy in LPFeeVault.collect(uint256) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#114-131):
	External calls:
	- (amount0,amount1) = positionManager.collect(INonfungiblePositionManager.CollectParams({tokenId:tokenId,recipient:address(this),amount0Max:type()(uint128).max,amount1Max:type()(uint128).max})) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#115-119)
	- (t0,c0) = _route(token0,amount0,creator,shareBps,vault) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#126)
		- ICreatorVault(vault).depositERC20(creator,token,creatorShare) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#147)
	- (t1,c1) = _route(token1,amount1,creator,shareBps,vault) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#127)
		- ICreatorVault(vault).depositERC20(creator,token,creatorShare) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#147)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- FeesCollected(tokenId,amount0,amount1) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#129)
	- FeesSplit(tokenId,creator,t0,c0,t1,c1) (src/LPFeeVault.sol#130)
disposition: FALSE-POSITIVE (§12.69). collect() emits FeesCollected/FeesSplit AFTER the harvest+split external calls, but every callee is trusted and callback-free: NPM.collect (trusted immutable §12.28); the launch token (plain OZ ERC20, no transfer hook) and canonical WETH9 (balance-mapping transfer, no recipient hook) for the treasury push; and OUR non-reverting CreatorVault.depositERC20 (pull + storage-accumulate, no callback back to the vault) for the creator share. No reentry into collect on the legit path; a donated hostile-token position (creatorOf==0) routes 100% to treasury with creatorShare==0 and can corrupt nothing (no standing vault balance; set-once creatorOf). Event ordering affects logs only. Same class as the existing reentrancy-events dispositions.

description:
BondingCurve.buy(address,address,address,uint256) (src/BondingCurve.sol#174-267) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- block.timestamp < EARLY_WINDOW_END && grossIn > MAX_EARLY_BUY (src/BondingCurve.sol#187)
disposition: REQUIRED BY SPEC — NOT A VULN. BondingCurve.buy compares `block.timestamp < EARLY_WINDOW_END` for the anti-sniper window. Using block.timestamp is MANDATED: block.number is banned on Orbit (spec §2/§6.5, CLAUDE.md hard rule). ~100ms blocks vs a seconds-scale window make sub-second sequencer skew immaterial; the acknowledged bypass is multi-wallet, not timestamp gaming (contracts.md §5.2).
